request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. For each candidate, decide if it is "useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge". Accept if useful and grounded; reject otherwise. Reject if it's legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence.

We must output JSON with verdicts for all candidates. Each verdict includes candidate_id, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge (boolean), reason (short), and category_if_not (one of the allowed values, or null if valid).

Let's go through each candidate:

1. cand_0a59c777f8430664: "Control Loop Enable/Disable Based on System Status" - Section 5.1.2, summary and evidence quote match. This is operational guidance from Guideline 36. Valid.

2. cand_b966bd1bcfc88805: "Control Loop Initialization on Enable/Reenable" - Section 5.1.3, quote matches. Valid.

3. cand_ed1dfcd99a9fac19: "Neutral Control Loop Condition" - Section 5.1.4, quote matches. Valid.

4. cand_a29e9dcf7d0f779d: "Outdoor Air Temperature Sensor Selection" - Section 5.1.5, quote matches. Valid.

5. cand_d0116cd61559429a: "Outdoor Air Temperature Sensor Validity for Air-Handler Intakes" - Section 5.1.5.1, quote matches. Valid.

6. cand_06cc073a564c3e88: "Outdoor Air Temperature Averaging for Global Sequences" - Section 5.1.5.2, quote matches. Valid.

7. cand_f08695ea0904329c: "Proven ON/OFF Definition" - Section 5.1.6, but the evidence quote is: "and differential pressure [DP] control , see Section 5.16.4 .)" which is clearly not the correct quote for a definition of Proven ON/OFF. The summary says it means DI status matches DO command. But the evidence is from page 20 and seems unrelated. This appears to be a mismatched extraction, possibly noisy. The candidate is likely unsupported by the provided evidence quote. Let's check the evidence: "and differential pressure [DP] control , see Section 5.16.4 .)" That's not about proven ON/OFF. So this is invalid because the evidence doesn't support the summary. It might be a duplicate or noisy extraction. Category: unsupported? Actually, the candidate_id might have been incorrectly assigned evidence. The guideline likely does define Proven ON/OFF somewhere (maybe 5.1.6), but the evidence quote provided is wrong. So we must reject based on the evidence provided. Category: unsupported (since the evidence doesn't match the claim). Or perhaps duplicate? But it's more unsupported. So is_valid_guideline36_knowledge: false, reason: "Evidence quote does not match the definition; unsupported extraction", category_if_not: "unsupported"

8. cand_7a4ffe1a5d7b1449: "Proportional-Only Loops for Limiting Loops" - Section 5.1.8.1, quote: "Use proportional only (P-only) loops for limiting loops (such as zone CO2 control loops, etc.)." This is valid operational guidance.

9. cand_577bd3e2a5c30653: "Derivative Term Usage Restriction" - Section 5.1.8.2, quote matches. Valid.

10. cand_d8e9a664335e9ddf: "Control Loop Output Rate of Change Limit" - Section 5.1.9, quote matches. Valid.

11. cand_b6d0066caf9b4189: "Adjustability of Setpoints, Timers, Deadbands, PID Gains" - Section 5.1.10, quote matches. Valid.

12. cand_f944a09a864a7a84: "Override Capability for Points" - Section 5.1.11, quote matches. Valid.

13. cand_f83ac219f4cfdab9: "Alarm Configuration Requirements" - Section 5.1.12, quote: "Each alarm can be configured in terms of level, latching (Requires Acknowledgment of a Return to Normal/Does Not Require Acknowledgment of a Return to Normal), entry delay, exit deadband, and postsuppression period." This is a parameter spec, valid.

14. cand_031cdcfeba03deb7: "Alarm Levels Definition" - Section 5.1.12.1, quote matches. Valid.

15. cand_e79a3a0d235cd09b: "Maintenance Mode Alarm Suppression" - Section 5.1.12.2, quote matches. Valid.

16. cand_163660721770e363: "Alarm Exit Hysteresis (Time-Based)" - Section 5.1.12.3, quote: "Each alarm shall have an adjustable time-based hysteresis (default: 5 seconds) to exit the alarm. Once set, the alarm does not return to normal until the alarm conditions have ceased for the duration of the hysteresis." This is valid.

17. cand_15c950861ea11e72: "Alarm Exit Hysteresis (Percent-of-Limit-Based)" - Section 5.1.12.3, evidence quote: "5.1.12.3.  Exit Hysteresis" That's just a section heading, not the content. The summary says it's about percent-of-limit-based hysteresis. But the evidence quote is insufficient—it's just the section number. This is likely a duplicate of the previous alarm hysteresis item? Actually, the previous one was time-based, this one is percent-of-limit-based. The evidence quote is not the actual text. So it's an unsupported extraction. Invalid. Category: unsupported.

18. cand_71d40bdfa4ea1f7d: "Alarm Latching Defaults" - Section 5.1.12.4, evidence: "5.1.12.4.  Latching . A latching alarm requires acknowledgment  from the operators before it can return to". This is incomplete, but the summary says default latching status: Level 1 and 2 latching, Level 3 and 4 nonlatching. The evidence quote is cut off. It might still be supported if the full text includes that. But the provided evidence quote is only a fragment. The candidate might still be valid if the evidence is enough? However, the quote is truncated and doesn't contain the defaults listed. So it's insufficient. Possibly the extraction is noisy. I'd say invalid because the evidence doesn't support the specific defaults. Category: unsupported.

19. cand_227d3540a8daffb0: "Post-Exit Suppression Period Defaults" - Section 5.1.12.5, quote: "Default suppression periods are as follows: a. Level 1 alarms: 0 minutes b. Level 2 alarms: 5 minutes c. Level 3 alarms: 24 hours d. Level 4 alarms: 7 days". This is fully supported. Valid.

20. cand_0253f64f5013482b: "VFD Speed Point Configuration" - Section 5.1.13, quote matches. Valid.

21. cand_c502118bc933792a: "VFD Minimum Speed Synchronization" - Section 5.1.13.2, evidence: "5.1.13.2. For each piece o f equipment, the minimum speed shall be stored in a single software point; in the". This is incomplete. The summary includes actions like reading every 60 minutes, mismatch detection, alarm. The evidence quote is cut off and doesn't show those details. Likely unsupported. Invalid. Category: unsupported.

22. cand_876284c4f4dbddf6: "Parallel Equipment Lead/Lag Rotation" - Section 5.1.15.1, quote: "Parallel equipment shall be lead/lag or lead/standby rotated to maintain even wear." Valid.

23. cand_241f91d6ca390545: "Runtime Points Definition" - Section 5.1.15.2, evidence: "5.1.15.2.  Two runtime points shall be defined for each equipment:" This is just a section heading/partial. The summary says Lifetime Runtime and Staging Runtime. The evidence quote is insufficient. Invalid. Category: unsupported.

24. cand_6249472d5ed1ca61: "Lead/Lag Alternation Based on Staging Runtime" - Section 5.1.15.3, quote matches. Valid.

25. cand_53a563bd98fb493a: "Lead/Standby Alternation for Redundant Equipment" - Section 5.1.15.4, quote matches. Valid.

26. cand_4108a71b78dd9e9a: "Lead/Standby Switch for Continuously Running Equipment" - Section 5.1.15.4, quote matches. Valid.

27. cand_45a17eba80e8ad47: "Variable Speed Fan/Pump Deceleration Rate" - Section 5.1.15.4, quote matches. Valid.

28. cand_91cfe67210231c77: "Operator Override of Staging Order" - Section 5.1.15.5, quote matches. Valid.

29. cand_e70de47cae6a79ab: "Faulted Equipment Detection for Fans and Pumps" - Section 5.1.15.5, quote: "Status point not matching its on/off point for 3 seconds after a time delay of 15 seconds while the equipment is commanded on." This is a fault diagnostic rule. Valid.

30. cand_a54c160d72187ca1: "Hand Operation Detection for Fans and Pumps" - Section 5.1.15.5, quote: "Status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF." Valid.

31. cand_98e5ef9fd65d04bd: "Hand Operation Response for Fans and Pumps" - Section 5.1.15.5, quote: "If a piece of equipment is ON-in-hand (e.g., via an HOA switch or local control of VFD), the equipment shall be set to the lead device, and a Level 4 alarm shall be generated. The equipment will remain as lead until." The quote is cut, but the summary matches. It seems sufficient. Valid.

32. cand_e9f53d34f69c3962: "Economizer Disable Based on High Limit" - Section 5.1.17.1, evidence: "5.1.17.1.  Economizer shall be disabled whenever the outdoor air conditions exceed the economizer high -" Incomplete. Summary says setpoints automatically determined. Evidence is insufficient. Invalid. Category: unsupported.

33. cand_410fe841eaafa7c0: "ASHRAE 90.1-2019 Economizer High Limits" - Section 5.1.17.2, evidence is a table. This is a parameter spec with values. Valid.

34. cand_01805303c5c5831e: "Title 24-2019 Economizer High Limits" - Section 5.1.17.3, evidence is a table. Valid.

35. cand_2d02ea86166c6529: "Damper/Valve Position Determination for T&R Requests" - Section 5.1.18.1, quote: "Knowledge of damper and valve position are required for proper generation of T&R reset requests." This is operational knowledge. Valid.

36. cand_67d5afea28ad1c8d: "Hierarchical Alarm Suppression Principle" - Section 5.1.19, quote: "The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load." Valid.

37. cand_4db998cb627fe387: "SystemOK Definition" - Section 5.1.19.3, quote matches. Valid.

38. cand_d7afd0c067ce42b6: "SystemOK FALSE Conditions" - Section 5.1.19.4, evidence: "5.1.19.4.  SystemOK shall be FALSE  while the system is starting up (i.e., before reaching setpoint ) or when" incomplete. Summary says: "SystemOK shall be FALSE while the system is starting up or when enough components are unavailable to disrupt service. By default, Level 1 through Level 3 component alarms inhibit SystemOK; Level 4 alarms do not." The evidence quote is cut off. Likely the full text includes the rest. But as provided, it's insufficient. Invalid. Category: unsupported.

39. cand_e97ec76a23f50b1a: "Selective Alarm Suppression by System Type" - Section 5.1.19.5, quote matches. Valid.

40. cand_0cdcfc093bc41447: "Alarms Never Suppressed by Hierarchical Logic" - Section 5.1.19.7, quote matches. Valid.

41. cand_ccd1cbc00b3fa21b: "Time-Based Suppression Delay After Setpoint Change" - Section 5.1.20.1, evidence: "5.1.20.1.  Calculate a time -delay period after any change in setpoint  based on the difference between the" incomplete. Summary includes defaults: 18 min per °C, max 120 min. Not supported by the truncated quote. Invalid. Category: unsupported.

So overall, the invalid ones are: cand_f08695ea0904329c, cand_15c950861ea11e72, cand_71d40bdfa4ea1f7d, cand_c502118bc933792a, cand_241f91d6ca390545, cand_e9f53d34f69c3962, cand_d7afd0c067ce42b6, cand_ccd1cbc00b3fa21b. Others are valid.

Now, output JSON.
